name: search_dashboards
description: Search for Grafana dashboards by a query string. Returns a list of matching dashboards with details like title, UID, folder, tags, and URL.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (max 100)

name: search_folders
description: Search for Grafana folders by a query string. Returns matching folders with details like title, UID, and URL.

name: get_dashboard_by_uid
description: Retrieves the complete dashboard, including panels, variables, and settings, for a specific dashboard identified by its UID. WARNING: Large dashboards can consume significant context window space. Consider using get_dashboard_summary for overview or get_dashboard_property for specific data instead.

name: update_dashboard
description: Create or update a dashboard. Two modes: (1) Full JSON — provide 'dashboard' for new dashboards or complete replacements. (2) Patch — provide 'uid' + 'operations' to make targeted changes to an existing dashboard. Patch operations support JSONPaths like '$.panels[0].targets[0].expr', '$.panels[1].title'. Append to arrays with '/- ' syntax. Multiple removes on the same array are automatically reordered.

dashboard.description: The full dashboard JSON. Use for creating new dashboards or complete updates.

uid.description: UID of existing dashboard to update. Must be used together with 'operations'.

operations.description: Array of patch operations for targeted updates.

path.description: JSONPath to the property to modify

folderUid.description: The UID of the dashboard's folder

message.description: Set a commit message for the version history

overwrite.description: Overwrite the dashboard if it exists. Otherwise create one

userId.description: ID of the user making the change

name: get_dashboard_panel_queries
description: Retrieve panel queries from a Grafana dashboard. Supports all datasource types (Prometheus, Loki, CloudWatch, SQL, etc.) and row-nested panels. Optionally filter to a specific panel by ID. Optionally provide variables for template variable substitution.

panelId.description: Optional panel ID to filter to a specific panel

name: get_dashboard_property
description: Get specific parts of a dashboard using JSONPath expressions to minimize context window usage. Common paths: '$.title' (title), '$.panels[*].title' (all panel titles), '$.panels[0]' (first panel), '$.templating.list' (variables). Use this instead of get_dashboard_by_uid when you only need specific dashboard properties.

jsonPath.description: JSONPath expression to extract specific data (e.g., '$.panels[0].title')

name: get_dashboard_summary
description: Get a compact summary of a dashboard including title, panel count, panel types, variables, and other metadata without the full JSON. Use this for dashboard overview and planning modifications without consuming large context windows.

name: list_datasources
description: List all configured datasources in Grafana. Use this to discover available datasources and their UIDs. Supports filtering by type and pagination.

type.description: The type of datasources to search for. For example, 'prometheus', 'loki', 'tempo', etc.

limit.description: Maximum number of datasources to return (max 100)

offset.description: Number of datasources to skip for pagination

name: get_datasource
description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific datasource by UID or name. Returns the full datasource model, including name, type, URL, access settings, JSON data, and secure JSON field status. Provide either uid or name; uid takes priority if both are given.

uid.description: The UID of the datasource. If provided, takes priority over name.

name.description: The name of the datasource. Used if UID is not provided.

name: get_annotations
description: Fetch Grafana annotations using filters such as dashboard UID, time range and tags.

matchAny.description: true OR tag match false AND

name: create_annotation
description: Create a new annotation on a dashboard or panel. Set format to 'graphite' and provide 'what' for Graphite-format annotations.

text.description: Annotation text (required unless format is graphite)

format.description: Set to 'graphite' to create a Graphite-format annotation

what.description: Annotation text for Graphite format (required when format is graphite)

when.description: Epoch ms timestamp for Graphite format

graphiteData.description: Optional string payload for Graphite format

name: update_annotation
description: Updates the provided properties of an annotation by ID. Only fields included in the request are modified; omitted fields are left unchanged.

name: get_annotation_tags
description: Returns annotation tags with optional filtering by tag name. Only the provided filters are applied.

name: alerting_manage_rules
description: Manage Grafana alert rules with filtering capabilities. Operations: 'list' to search/filter rules, 'get' to retrieve full rule details by UID, 'versions' to get version history, 'create' to create a new rule, 'update' to update an existing rule, 'delete' to delete a rule. When NOT to use: checking how alerts are routed to receivers (use alerting_manage_routing).

rule_uid.description: The UID of the alert rule (required for get, versions, update, delete)

rule_limit.description: Maximum number of rules to return (default 200, max 200)

datasource_uid.description: Optional: UID of a Prometheus or Loki datasource to query for datasource-managed alert rules

label_selectors.description: Label matchers to filter alert rules

limit_alerts.description: Limit alert instances per rule. 0 omits alerts.

folder_uid.description: Filter by exact folder UID (list) or folder to store rule in (create/update)

search_folder.description: Search folders by path using partial matching (list only)

title.description: The title of the alert rule (required for create, update)

rule_group.description: The rule group name (required for create, update)

condition.description: The query condition identifier, e.g. 'A', 'B' (required for create, update)

data.description: Array of alert query objects (required for create and update)

no_data_state.description: State when no data (NoData, Alerting, OK)

exec_err_state.description: State on execution error (NoData, Alerting, OK)

disableProvenance.description: If true, the alert remains editable in the Grafana UI. Defaults to true.

name: alerting_manage_routing
description: Manage Grafana alerting routing configuration, including notification policies, contact points and time intervals. When NOT to use: checking alert rule configuration or state (use alerting_manage_rules).

datasource_uid.description: Optional: UID of an Alertmanager-compatible datasource to query for receivers. Only used with get_contact_points.

name.description: Filter contact points by name (exact match). Only used with get_contact_points.

contact_point_title.description: Title of the contact point to retrieve (required for get_contact_point operation)

time_interval_name.description: Name of the time interval to retrieve (required for get_time_interval operation)

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return. Default is 100. Only used with get_contact_points.

name: list_prometheus_metric_metadata
description: List Prometheus metric metadata. Returns metadata about metrics currently scraped from targets. Note: This endpoint is experimental.

datasourceUid.description: The UID of the datasource to query

limit.description: The maximum number of metrics to return

limitPerMetric.description: The maximum number of metrics to return per metric

name: query_prometheus
description: Query a PromQL-compatible datasource (Prometheus, Thanos, Mimir, Cloud Monitoring, etc.) using a PromQL expression. Supports instant queries (single point) and range queries (time range). Time: RFC3339 or relative expressions like 'now', 'now-1h'. WORKFLOW: list_prometheus_metric_names -> list_prometheus_label_values -> query_prometheus.

datasourceUid.description: The UID of the datasource to query

startTime.description: The start time. Supported formats are RFC3339 or relative to now (e.g. 'now', 'now-1.5h', 'now-2h45m')

endTime.description: The end time. Required if queryType is 'range'

stepSeconds.description: The time series step size in seconds. Required if queryType is 'range'

queryType.description: The type of query to use. Either 'range' or 'instant'

name: query_prometheus_histogram
description: Query Prometheus histogram percentiles. Generates histogram_quantile PromQL. DISCOVER FIRST: Use list_prometheus_metric_names with regex='.*_bucket$' to find histograms. Example: metric='http_duration', percentile=95, labels='job="api"'. Time formats: 'now-1h', '2026-02-02T19:00:00Z', Unix ms.

datasourceUid.description: The UID of the Prometheus datasource

stepSeconds.description: Step size in seconds for range query (default: 60)

name: list_prometheus_metric_names
description: DISCOVERY: Call this first to find available metrics before querying. Lists metric names in a PromQL-compatible datasource. Retrieves all metric names and filters them using the provided regex. Supports pagination.

datasourceUid.description: The UID of the datasource to query

regex.description: The regex to match against the metric names

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return

name: list_prometheus_label_names
description: List label names in a PromQL-compatible datasource (Prometheus, Thanos, Mimir, Cloud Monitoring, etc.). Allows filtering by series selectors and time range.

datasourceUid.description: The UID of the datasource to query

matches.description: Optionally, a list of label matchers to filter the results by

startRfc3339.description: The start time of the time range to filter the results by

endRfc3339.description: The end time of the time range to filter the results by

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return

name: list_prometheus_label_values
description: Use after list_prometheus_metric_names to find label values for filtering queries. Gets the values for a specific label name in a PromQL-compatible datasource. Allows filtering by series selectors and time range.

datasourceUid.description: The UID of the datasource to query

labelName.description: The name of the label to query

matches.description: Optionally, a list of selectors to filter the results by

startRfc3339.description: The start time of the query

endRfc3339.description: The end time of the query

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return

name: list_loki_label_names
description: Lists all available label names (keys) found in logs within a specified Loki datasource and time range. Returns a list of unique label strings. If the time range is not provided, it defaults to the last hour.

datasourceUid.description: The UID of the datasource to query

startRfc3339.description: The start time of the query in RFC3339 format (defaults to 1 hour ago)

endRfc3339.description: The end time of the query in RFC3339 format (defaults to now)

name: list_loki_label_values
description: Retrieves all unique values associated with a specific labelName within a Loki datasource and time range. Useful for discovering filter options. Defaults to the last hour if the time range is omitted.

datasourceUid.description: The UID of the datasource to query

labelName.description: The name of the label to retrieve values for (e.g. 'app', 'env', 'pod')

startRfc3339.description: The start time of the query in RFC3339 format (defaults to 1 hour ago)

endRfc3339.description: The end time of the query in RFC3339 format (defaults to now)

name: query_loki_logs
description: Executes a LogQL query against a Loki datasource to retrieve log entries or metric values. Returns a list of results, each containing a timestamp, labels, and either a log line or a numeric metric value. Defaults to the last hour, a limit of 10 entries, and 'backward' direction (newest first). Supports full LogQL syntax.

datasourceUid.description: The UID of the datasource to query

logql.description: The LogQL query to execute against Loki. Supports full LogQL syntax including label matchers, filter operators, pattern expressions, and pipeline operations.

startRfc3339.description: The start time of the query in RFC3339 format

endRfc3339.description: The end time of the query in RFC3339 format

limit.description: The maximum number of log lines to return (default max: 100)

direction.description: The direction of the query: 'forward' (oldest first) or 'backward' (newest first, default)

stepSeconds.description: Resolution step in seconds for range metric queries.

name: query_loki_stats
description: Retrieves statistics about log streams matching a given LogQL selector within a Loki datasource and time range. Returns streams, chunks, entries, and total bytes. The logql parameter must be a simple label selector (e.g., {app="nginx"}). Defaults to the last hour if the time range is omitted.

datasourceUid.description: The UID of the datasource to query

logql.description: The LogQL matcher expression. Must be a simple label selector only.

startRfc3339.description: The start time of the query in RFC3339 format

endRfc3339.description: The end time of the query in RFC3339 format

name: query_loki_patterns
description: Retrieves detected log patterns from a Loki datasource for a given stream selector and time range. Returns a list of patterns, each containing a pattern string and a total count of occurrences. The logql parameter must be a stream selector. Defaults to the last hour if the time range is omitted.

datasourceUid.description: The UID of the datasource to query

logql.description: A LogQL stream selector to identify the logs to analyze for patterns (e.g. {job="foo"})

startRfc3339.description: The start time of the query in RFC3339 format (defaults to 1 hour ago)

endRfc3339.description: The end time of the query in RFC3339 format (defaults to now)

name: list_incidents
description: List Grafana incidents. Allows filtering by status ('active', 'resolved') and optionally including drill incidents. Returns a preview list with basic details.

limit.description: The maximum number of incidents to return

status.description: The status of the incidents to include. Valid values: 'active', 'resolved'

name: create_incident
description: Create a new Grafana incident. Requires title, severity, and room prefix. Allows setting status and labels. This tool should be used judiciously and sparingly, and only after confirmation from the user, as it may notify or alarm lots of people.

roomPrefix.description: The prefix of the room to create the incident in

isDrill.description: Whether the incident is a drill incident

labels.description: The labels to add to the incident

name: add_activity_to_incident
description: Add a note (userNote activity) to an existing incident's timeline using its ID. The note body can include URLs which will be attached as context. Use this to add context to an incident.

incidentId.description: The ID of the incident to add the activity to

body.description: The body of the activity. URLs will be parsed and attached as context

eventTime.description: The time that the activity occurred. If not provided, the current time will be used

name: get_incident
description: Get a single incident by ID. Returns the full incident details including title, status, severity, labels, timestamps, and other metadata.

id.description: The ID of the incident to retrieve

name: list_teams
description: Search for Grafana teams by a query string. Returns a list of matching teams with details like name, member count, email, and avatar URL.

query.description: The query to search for teams. Can be left empty to fetch all teams

name: list_users_by_org
description: List users in the Grafana organization. Returns a list of organization users with details like userId, login, email, and role.

name: list_all_roles
description: List all roles in Grafana. Optionally filter to show only roles that can be delegated by the current user.

delegatableOnly.description: Optional: If set true only return roles that can be delegated by current user

name: get_role_details
description: Get detailed information about a specific Grafana role by its UID, including permissions, metadata, and assignment details.

name: get_role_assignments
description: List all assignments for a specific role, showing which users, teams, and service accounts have been assigned this role.

name: list_user_roles
description: List all roles assigned to one or more users. Returns a map of user IDs to their assigned roles.

userIds.description: User ID(s) to get roles for.

name: list_team_roles
description: List all roles assigned to one or more teams. Returns a map of team IDs to their assigned roles.

teamIds.description: Team ID(s) to get roles for.

name: get_resource_permissions
description: List all permissions set on a specific Grafana resource (e.g., dashboard, datasource, folder) by its type and unique identifier.

resourceId.description: Unique identifier of the resource (UID for dashboards/datasources/folders)

name: get_resource_description
description: List available permissions and assignment capabilities for a Grafana resource type.

resourceType.description: Type of Grafana resource to get description for

name: create_folder
description: Create a Grafana folder. Provide a title and optional UID. Returns the created folder.

uid.description: Optional folder UID. If omitted, Grafana will generate one.

parentUid.description: Optional parent folder UID. If set, the folder will be created under this parent.

name: get_panel_image
description: Render a Grafana dashboard panel or full dashboard as a PNG image. Returns the image as base64 encoded data. Requires the Grafana Image Renderer service to be installed.

dashboardUid.description: The UID of the dashboard containing the panel

panelId.description: The ID of the panel to render. If omitted, the entire dashboard is rendered

width.description: Width of the rendered image in pixels. Defaults to 1000

height.description: Height of the rendered image in pixels. Defaults to 500

timeRange.description: Time range for the rendered image

name: generate_deeplink
description: Generate deeplink URLs for Grafana resources. Supports dashboards (requires dashboardUid), panels (requires dashboardUid and panelId), and Explore queries (requires datasourceUid). Optionally accepts time range and additional query parameters.

resourceType.description: Type of resource: dashboard, panel, or explore

dashboardUid.description: Dashboard UID (required for dashboard and panel types)

datasourceUid.description: Datasource UID (required for explore type)

name: run_panel_query
description: Executes one or more dashboard panel queries with optional time range and variable overrides. Accepts an array of panel IDs to query in a single call. Fetches the dashboard, extracts queries from the specified panels, substitutes template variables and Grafana macros, and routes to the appropriate datasource. Returns results keyed by panel ID.

panelIds.description: Panel IDs to execute (one or more)

queryIndex.description: Index of the query to execute per panel (0-based, defaults to 0)

start.description: Override start time (e.g. 'now-1h', RFC3339, Unix ms)

end.description: Override end time (e.g. 'now', RFC3339, Unix ms)

datasourceUid.description: Override datasource UID if panel uses a template variable datasource

name: search_logs
description: Search for log entries matching a text pattern across ClickHouse and Loki datasources.

datasourceUid.description: The UID of a ClickHouse or Loki datasource

pattern.description: Text pattern or regex to search for in log messages

table.description: Table name for ClickHouse queries. DISCOVERY REQUIRED: Run list_clickhouse_tables first. Default 'otel_logs'. Ignored for Loki.

start.description: Start time (e.g. 'now-1h', '2026-02-02T19:00:00Z', Unix ms). Defaults to 'now-1h'

end.description: End time (e.g. 'now', RFC3339, Unix ms). Defaults to 'now'

limit.description: Maximum number of log entries to return (max 1000)

name: query_elasticsearch
description: Executes a search query against an Elasticsearch datasource and retrieves matching documents. Supports both Lucene query syntax and Elasticsearch Query DSL JSON. Returns a list of documents with their index, ID, source fields, and optional score. Defaults to 10 results and sorts by @timestamp in descending order.

datasourceUid.description: The UID of the Elasticsearch datasource to query

index.description: The index pattern to search (e.g., 'logs-*', 'filebeat-*', or a specific index name)

query.description: The search query. Can be Lucene query syntax or Elasticsearch Query DSL JSON

startTime.description: The start time in RFC3339 format

endTime.description: The end time in RFC3339 format

limit.description: The maximum number of documents to return (max: 100, default: 10)

name: query_clickhouse
description: Query ClickHouse via Grafana. REQUIRED FIRST: Use list_clickhouse_tables to find tables, then describe_clickhouse_table to see column schemas, then query.

datasourceUid.description: The UID of the ClickHouse datasource to query. Use list_datasources to find available UIDs.

query.description: Raw SQL query. Supports ClickHouse macros: $__timeFilter(column) for time filtering, $__from/$__to for millisecond timestamps, $__interval/$__interval_ms for calculated intervals.

startTime.description: Start time for time filter macros (e.g. 'now-1h', RFC3339)

endTime.description: End time for time filter macros (e.g. 'now', RFC3339)

maxDataPoints.description: Max data points for interval calculations (default: 1000)

name: list_clickhouse_tables
description: START HERE for ClickHouse: List available tables (name, database, engine, row count, size). NEXT: Use describe_clickhouse_table to see column schemas.

datasourceUid.description: The UID of the ClickHouse datasource

database.description: Database name to filter tables (lists all non-system databases if not specified)

name: describe_clickhouse_table
description: Get column schema for a ClickHouse table. Pass the database from list_clickhouse_tables results. NEXT: Use query_clickhouse with discovered column names.

datasourceUid.description: The UID of the ClickHouse datasource

name: query_cloudwatch
description: Query AWS CloudWatch metrics via Grafana. Requires region. WORKFLOW: list_cloudwatch_namespaces -> list_cloudwatch_metrics -> list_cloudwatch_dimensions -> query_cloudwatch.

datasourceUid.description: The UID of the CloudWatch datasource to query

namespace.description: CloudWatch namespace (e.g. AWS/ECS, AWS/EC2, AWS/RDS, AWS/Lambda)

statistic.description: Statistics to return (e.g. Average, Sum, Maximum, Minimum, SampleCount)

accountId.description: AWS account ID for cross-account monitoring

name: list_cloudwatch_namespaces
description: START HERE for CloudWatch: List available namespaces (AWS/EC2, AWS/ECS, AWS/RDS, etc.). Requires region.

datasourceUid.description: The UID of the CloudWatch datasource

accountId.description: AWS account ID for cross-account monitoring, or 'all' for all linked accounts.

name: list_cloudwatch_metrics
description: List metrics for a CloudWatch namespace. Requires region. Supports cross-account monitoring via optional accountId.

datasourceUid.description: The UID of the CloudWatch datasource

accountId.description: AWS account ID for cross-account monitoring

name: list_cloudwatch_dimensions
description: List dimension keys for a CloudWatch metric. Requires region. Supports cross-account monitoring via optional accountId.

datasourceUid.description: The UID of the CloudWatch datasource

accountId.description: AWS account ID for cross-account monitoring

name: list_pyroscope_label_names
description: List label names available in a Pyroscope datasource.

data_source_uid.description: The UID of the datasource to query

matchers.description: Prometheus style matchers used to filter the result set

name: list_pyroscope_label_values
description: List values for a specific label in a Pyroscope datasource.

data_source_uid.description: The UID of the datasource to query

matchers.description: Optionally, Prometheus style matchers used to filter the result set

name: list_pyroscope_profile_types
description: List available profile types in a Pyroscope datasource.

data_source_uid.description: The UID of the datasource to query

start_rfc_3339.description: The start time of the query in RFC3339 format (defaults to 1 hour ago)

end_rfc_3339.description: The end time of the query in RFC3339 format (defaults to now)

name: fetch_pyroscope_profile
description: Fetch a profiling flame graph from a Pyroscope datasource for a given profile type and label matchers.

data_source_uid.description: The UID of the datasource to query

profile_type.description: Type profile type, use the list_pyroscope_profile_types tool to fetch available profile types

matchers.description: Optionally, Prometheus style matchers used to filter the result set

start_rfc_3339.description: The start time of the query in RFC3339 format (defaults to 1 hour ago)

end_rfc_3339.description: The end time of the query in RFC3339 format (defaults to now)

name: list_oncall_schedules
description: List Grafana OnCall schedules, optionally filtering by team ID. If a specific schedule ID is provided, returns only that schedule's details.

teamId.description: The ID of the team to list schedules for

scheduleId.description: The ID of the schedule to get details for

name: get_oncall_shift
description: Get detailed information for a specific Grafana OnCall shift using its ID.

shiftId.description: The ID of the shift to get details for

name: get_current_oncall_users
description: Get the list of users currently on-call for a specific Grafana OnCall schedule ID.

scheduleId.description: The ID of the schedule to get current on-call users for

name: list_oncall_teams
description: List teams configured in Grafana OnCall. Returns a list of team objects with their details. Supports pagination.

name: list_oncall_users
description: List users from Grafana OnCall. These are OnCall users (separate from Grafana users). Can retrieve a specific user by ID or username, or list all users with pagination.

userId.description: The ID of the user to get details for. If provided, returns only that user's details

username.description: The username to filter users by

name: list_alert_groups
description: List alert groups from Grafana OnCall with filtering options. Supports filtering by alert group ID, route ID, integration ID, state, team ID, and time range.

id.description: Filter by specific alert group ID

state.description: Filter by alert group state (one of: new, acknowledged, resolved, silenced)

name: get_alert_group
description: Get a specific alert group from Grafana OnCall by its ID. Returns the full alert group details.

alertGroupId.description: The ID of the alert group to retrieve

name: get_sift_investigation
description: Retrieves an existing Sift investigation by its UUID.

id.description: The UUID of the investigation as a string (e.g. '02adab7c-bf5b-45f2-9459-d71a2c29e11b')

name: get_sift_analysis
description: Retrieves a specific analysis from an investigation by its UUID.

analysisId.description: The UUID of the specific analysis to retrieve

name: list_sift_investigations
description: Retrieves a list of Sift investigations with an optional limit. If no limit is specified, defaults to 10.

limit.description: Maximum number of investigations to return

name: find_error_pattern_logs
description: Searches Loki logs for elevated error patterns compared to the last day's average, waits for the analysis to complete, and returns the result.

start.description: Start time for the investigation. Defaults to 30 minutes ago if not specified.

end.description: End time for the investigation. Defaults to now if not specified.

name: find_slow_requests
description: Searches relevant Tempo datasources for slow requests, waits for the analysis to complete, and returns the result.

start.description: Start time for the investigation. Defaults to 30 minutes ago if not specified.

end.description: End time for the investigation. Defaults to now if not specified.
